key_insight: A polygonal cell-based smoothed finite element framework enables robust nonlinear geotechnical analysis on flexible meshes without requiring direct shape-function derivatives inside each element.
engineering_relevance: The method is well suited for staged construction, local failure, and complex soil–structure problems where accurate displacement, stress, plastic strain, and stability predictions are needed.
method: Uses Wachspress interpolation on polygonal elements.
Applies cell-based strain smoothing with boundary integration over polygonal subcells.
Includes incremental elasto-plastic updates for Mohr–Coulomb and Duncan–Chang soil models.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports verification on a perforated plate, strip footing, core rockfill dam, tunnel excavation, and slope stability cases, with accurate predictions of displacement, stress, plastic strain, bearing capacity, and factor of safety.
watch_item: Implementation relies on user-element integration in ABAQUS and may require careful handling of polygonal/hybrid meshes and constitutive update stability in highly nonlinear problems.
paper_title: Nonlinear Geotechnical Analysis Using a Polygonal Cell-Based Smoothed Finite Element Framework
